Operation 'post-updateAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on return values and side effects. Added explicit mention of the response type (UpdateAccountHolderResponse) and clarified that this operation both updates existing fields and adds new entities. Improved structure for AI agent understanding of when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description:
Updates the `accountHolderDetails` and `processingTier` of an account holder, and adds bank accounts and shareholders.

When updating `accountHolderDetails`, parameters that are not included in the request are left unchanged except for the following object:

* `metadata`: Updating the metadata replaces the entire object. This means that to update an existing key-value pair, you must provide the changes, as well as other existing key-value pairs.

When updating any field in the following objects, you must submit all the fields required for validation:

 * `address`

* `fullPhoneNumber`
 
* `bankAccountDetails.BankAccountDetail`

* `businessDetails.shareholders.ShareholderContact`
 
 For example, to update the `address.postalCode`, you must also submit the `address.country`, `.city`, `.street`, `.postalCode`, and possibly `.stateOrProvince` so that the address can be validated.

To add a bank account or shareholder, provide the bank account or shareholder details without a `bankAccountUUID` or a `shareholderCode`.
data.description_suggestion: Updates account holder details including accountHolderDetails, processingTier, bank accounts, and shareholders. Partial updates are supported for most fields, but metadata replacement is complete (provide all key-value pairs). When updating address, fullPhoneNumber, bankAccountDetails, or shareholders, all required validation fields must be submitted together. To add new bank accounts or shareholders, omit the bankAccountUUID or shareholderCode. Returns an UpdateAccountHolderResponse with the updated account holder information and status.
data.reasoning: The current description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on return values and side effects. Added explicit mention of the response type (UpdateAccountHolderResponse) and clarified that this operation both updates existing fields and adds new entities. Improved structure for AI agent understanding of when to use this operation and what to expect.

data.current_description: Disables or enables the processing or payout state of an account holder.
data.description_suggestion: Enables or disables the processing or payout state of an account holder. Specify the desired state (enabled/disabled) and state type (processing or payout) in the request. This operation controls whether the account holder can process transactions or receive payouts. Returns an UpdateAccountHolderStateResponse confirming the new state configuration.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks critical details. Added information about required inputs (state type and desired state), the operational effect (controls transaction processing and payout capability), and the expected response type. This helps AI agents understand the prerequisites and consequences of calling this operation.

data.current_description: Uploads a document for an account holder. Adyen uses the documents during the [verification process](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/verification-process).
data.description_suggestion: Uploads a verification document for an account holder to support the KYC verification process. The document is associated with the specified account holder and document type. Uploaded documents are used by Adyen during verification checks to validate account holder information and compliance requirements. Returns an UploadDocumentResponse confirming successful upload and document reference details.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs (account holder reference, document type) and outputs. Added context about document association, the verification workflow, and expected response. Clarified that this operation supports compliance and KYC processes, helping AI agents understand the business purpose and when to invoke this operation.

data.current_description: Deletes signatories associated with an account holder.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete signatories from an account holder
data.description_suggestion: Removes one or more signatories associated with an account holder. Requires the account holder reference and signatory identifiers in the request. Returns confirmation of deletion. This operation permanently removes the signatory records and affects the account holder's verification status.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific account holder, including personal information, account status, verification checks, and KYC results. data.current_description: Returns documents that were previously uploaded for an account holder. Adyen uses the documents during the [verification process](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/verification-process).
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all documents previously uploaded for a specific account holder. Requires the account holder reference as input. Returns a list of uploaded documents with metadata including document type, upload date, and verification status. These documents are used by Adyen during the account holder verification process.

data.current_description: Changes the [status of an account holder](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/account-holders-and-accounts#account-holder-statuses) to **Suspended**.
data.description_suggestion: Changes an account holder's status to Suspended, restricting their account activities. Requires the account holder reference as input. Returns the updated account holder object with the new Suspended status. This operation is typically used to temporarily restrict an account holder pending verification or compliance review.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. message: Operation 'post-unSuspendAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Current description is verbose with multiple links and lacks clarity on inputs/outputs. Enhanced version consolidates the key information: what the operation does, required inputs, response structure, preconditions (no failed verification checks), and context about how accounts become suspended. This improves AI agent interpretability while maintaining completeness.

data.current_description:
Changes the [status of an account holder](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/account-holders-and-accounts#account-holder-statuses) from **Suspended** to **Inactive**. 
Account holders can have a **Suspended** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status) if you suspend them through the [`/suspendAccountHolder`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/v5/post/suspendAccountHolder) endpoint or if a verification deadline expires.

You can only unsuspend account holders if they do not have verification checks with a **FAILED** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status).
data.description_suggestion: Changes an account holder's status from Suspended to Inactive, restoring limited account functionality. Requires the account holder reference as input. Returns the updated account holder object with the new Inactive status. This operation can only succeed if the account holder has no failed verification checks. Account holders become Suspended either through manual suspension via /suspendAccountHolder or when verification deadlines expire.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Current description is verbose with multiple links and lacks clarity on inputs/outputs. Enhanced version consolidates the key information: what the operation does, required inputs, response structure, preconditions (no failed verification checks), and context about how accounts become suspended. This improves AI agent interpretability while maintaining completeness.

message: Operation 'post-closeAccountHolder' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is generic. Description contains useful information but is verbose with markdown links and unclear about scope (affects all associated accounts). Improved summary is more specific about the status change action. Improved description clarifies the permanent nature, scope of impact on associated accounts, and expected return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Changes the [status of an account holder](https://docs.adyen.com/classic-platforms/account-holders-and-accounts#account-holder-statuses) to **Closed**. This state is final. If an account holder is closed, you can't process transactions, pay out funds, or reopen it. If payments are made to an account of an account holder with a **Closed** [`status`](https://docs.adyen.com/api-explorer/#/Account/latest/post/getAccountHolder__resParam_verification-accountHolder-checks-status), the payments are sent to your liable account.
data.summary_suggestion: Set account holder status to Closed
data.description_suggestion: Permanently closes an account holder by changing its status to Closed. Once closed, no transactions can be processed, funds cannot be paid out, and the account holder cannot be reopened. All associated accounts are affected. Payments received for closed account holders are redirected to your liable account. Returns the updated account holder status.
data.reasoning: Original summary is generic. Description contains useful information but is verbose with markdown links and unclear about scope (affects all associated accounts). Improved summary is more specific about the status change action. Improved description clarifies the permanent nature, scope of impact on associated accounts, and expected return value for better AI agent understanding.
